# FlagFit Pro — Source of Truth

**The single authoritative doc.** It replaces the prior ~33 hand-written docs (which drifted; see git history). Rule: where this file or the generated files conflict with old prose, **ground truth wins**. Ground truth, in priority order: live Supabase schema → actual routes/handlers → service code → applied migrations. Prose is not ground truth.

- **Data Model** (generated, exact live names): [`docs/generated/DATA_MODEL.md`](generated/DATA_MODEL.md)
- **Endpoint Reference** (generated): [`docs/generated/ENDPOINTS.md`](generated/ENDPOINTS.md)
- **Live schema snapshot** (the offline ground-truth input): [`docs/generated/live-schema.snapshot.json`](generated/live-schema.snapshot.json)

Regenerate the generated sections: `npm run docs:regen` (see §8).
Verified against live: **2026-07-08** (full rules/single-source-of-truth audit + the periodization-prescription endpoint, now switched on for `today`, via authenticated Supabase MCP) — 181 base tables, 7 views, 121 Netlify functions (117 exercised, 4 orphaned), 249 migration files in `supabase/migrations/`. The generated DATA_MODEL/ENDPOINTS, `live-schema.snapshot.json`, and `supabase-types.ts` were all regenerated 2026-07-08 (the snapshot had drifted since 2026-07-06 — see §6).

---

## 1. System Map

**Stack.** Angular 22 (standalone, zoneless, signals; NO PrimeNG — the static-first rebuild removed it) → built by Netlify → Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Realtime + Storage). API is **Netlify Functions** under `netlify/functions/*.js`, exposed at `/api/*` via `netlify.toml` redirects. Functions use the **ESM** `export const handler` signature (not `exports.handler`) and the shared service-role client in `netlify/functions/supabase-client.js`. There is **no `angular/src/app/features/` directory** — feature screens are direct children of `angular/src/app/` (today, wellness, training, stats, schedule, competition, roster, settings, onboarding, monitoring-report, …). Routing: `app/app.routes.ts` + `app/core/routes/feature-routes.ts`.

**Engine.** Readiness and ACWR/load are computed server-side and read-through by the client (Readiness: `netlify/functions/calc-readiness.js` → `readiness_scores`; ACWR: `compute-acwr.js` + `utils/acwr.js`, 7d acute / 21d chronic uncoupled EWMA, over `training_sessions`). The daily prescription (`today`) is computed by a **shared pure engine** (`periodization-engine`, mirrored client/server, proven byte-identical) — the server's `/api/periodization-prescription` is preferred once it verified-matches the client's own computation for that athlete/date; `weekAhead()` (7-day planning view) stays client-only by design. See §5a for the exact current-state nuance and Law #6. Schedule spine: `v_athlete_schedule` (union across team memberships + athlete-entered `athlete_events`).

**Lanes.** `/api/coach-*` and `/api/staff-*` (nutritionist/physiotherapist/psychology) are role-gated staff lanes; most athlete features hit the un-prefixed lanes. Many functions are **routers** that delegate to sub-modules via ESM import (e.g. `social.js` → `chat.js`/`community.js`; `training.js` → `training-*`); sub-modules have no own `/api` redirect but are reachable through the parent.

**Realtime.** `core/services/realtime.service.ts` is the central `postgres_changes` manager (subscribes `training_sessions`, `daily_wellness_checkin`, `readiness_scores`, `chat_messages`, `channels`, `notifications`, `team_members`, `coach_activity_log`, `games`, `messages`, `performance_metrics`). `acwr.service` also subscribes `training_sessions`; `channel.service` delegates to `realtime.service`. (Realtime only fires for tables in the `supabase_realtime` publication — verify before relying on it.)

**Auth.** Supabase Auth, localStorage + Bearer token (NOT cookies; no CSRF tokens). The route guard `core/guards/auth.guard.ts` is **config-gated** by `environment.auth.required` — open in dev/smoke, enforcing in prod. Only two guards exist: `auth.guard.ts`, `staff.guard.ts`.

## 2. Data Model

Generated, exact live names/columns/nullability, with which endpoints touch each table and a DRIFT flag for live tables lacking a migration file: **[`docs/generated/DATA_MODEL.md`](generated/DATA_MODEL.md)**. 181 base tables, 7 views (`physical_measurements_latest`, `user_achievements`, `v_athlete_schedule`, `v_injuries_unified`, `v_pending_event_participation`, `v_seed_integrity`, `v_training_sessions_consent`). Canonical entity names that have historically been mis-inferred: wellness = **`daily_wellness_checkin`** (not `wellness_checkins`/`wellness_logs`); injuries = **`athlete_injuries`** + `v_injuries_unified` (not `injuries`); load source = **`training_sessions`** (not `workout_logs`/`load_monitoring`).

> `supabase-types.ts` was regenerated from the live schema **2026-07-08** (via Supabase MCP `generate_typescript_types`) — 0 stale-in-types, 0 live-missing-from-types. The live snapshot remains the ground truth for the data model; regenerate types again after any schema change.

## 3. Endpoint Reference

Generated, every route with method / `/api` path / tables-and-RPCs touched / EXERCISED|ORPHANED: **[`docs/generated/ENDPOINTS.md`](generated/ENDPOINTS.md)**. 121 functions (117 exercised, 4 orphaned: `auth-reset-password`, `exercisedb`, `session-load-import`, `wearable-health-ingest` — the latter two are the monitoring-feature device/provider ingest lanes, designed for external callers with no Angular upload UI). `periodization-prescription` (the server-side prescription endpoint, §5a) is now called by `PeriodizationService` and correctly shows EXERCISED. Orphaned endpoints stay listed so nobody rebuilds them. As of 2026-07-08 **no function references a non-existent table** — the ghost-table `.from()` refs were retired (see §6).

## 5. Spec Laws (durable product rules)

1. **P0 — never advance past a failed critical write.** If a write that the next step depends on fails (onboarding profile, check-in, account action), surface the error and stop; do not navigate forward as if it succeeded.
2. **Physio/injury blocks override training prescriptions.** An active `athlete_injuries` / tightness signal takes precedence over the engine's prescribed session — the plan works around the flagged region.
3. **Nutrition is food-first.** Athlete-facing nutrition uses food/portion language, not g/kg notation.
4. **Answer-first Today ordering.** The Today screen leads with the day's answer (prescription + readiness), diagnostics below the fold.
5. **Onboarding delivers value before asking for complexity.** Show usefulness early; defer heavy data collection.
6. **Server is canonical for the engine.** Readiness/ACWR/prescription are computed server-side; the client renders and never re-derives. Missing data → explicit empty state, never a fabricated number. **Current exceptions (both narrow, tracked in §5a):** `today` prefers the server but falls back to a local computation when the server hasn't verified-matched yet for that athlete/date (a transitional safety gate, not a permanent divergence); `weekAhead()` (the 7-day planning view) remains fully client-computed by deliberate design, not a gap.
7. **No fabricated UI data.** Inputs prefill from real saved state; never show placeholder values that masquerade as the athlete's data.

### 5a. Recovery modalities & adaptive load (detailed law)

- **Current state (Law #6 now true for `today`; `weekAhead` remains a tracked exception):** the pure algorithm — `angular/src/app/core/services/periodization-engine.ts` (`prescribeFor`/`decideBasePrescription`, extracted 2026-07-07 from the former `periodization.service.ts`) — is mirrored server-side and both are wired together. Recovery and injury precedence are wired **in the engine** (client and server share the identical module), not in the `daily_protocols`/`ai-chat` generator (which remains for coach/AI features, not the athlete plan). **Backend-authoritative migration (2026-07-08):**
  - The engine is mirrored server-side (`netlify/functions/utils/periodization-engine.js`, generated via `npm run build:periodization-engine`) and proven **byte-for-byte identical** to the client on a 29-case golden-fixture matrix (`tests/unit/periodization-port-parity.test.js`).
  - A server endpoint (`/api/periodization-prescription`) assembles the same `PeriodizationInputs` the client wrapper builds — schedule/phase (`schedule.js`'s extracted `getScheduleSnapshot()`), ACWR + readiness (`readiness_scores`), injuries (`getActiveInjuries` + a restriction-resolver mirroring `InjuryService.restrictions()`), position/age/bodyweight/season-calendar/team-practice (`users`/`athlete_training_config`), recent sessions (CNS spacing), **weather** (reuses `weather.js`'s `resolveTeamHomeCity`/`getWeatherData`) and **travel/acclimatization** (`athlete_travel_log`, mirroring `EventTravelService` exactly) — then calls the ported engine. 31 unit tests lock in the assembly logic. Only `coachOverride`/`weeklyIntentHint`/`weeklyProgressionUnsafe` stay unwired (each the engine's own documented "no guard" default).
  - **`today` now prefers the server, with a verified-match gate:** `PeriodizationService.today` = `serverPrescription() ?? localPrescription()` — `localPrescription` is the unchanged client computation (still the instant, offline-safe first answer, satisfying Law #4); an effect fetches the server prescription for the same date, and **only adopts it into `serverPrescription` when its CORE fields (intent/targetRpe/targetMinutes/sprintReps/strengthSets) match the local computation exactly** — logged via `RemoteTelemetryService` to `frontend_logs` as `periodization_server_match`/`periodization_server_mismatch`. A mismatch is NOT adopted (day-one-of-rollout policy: the engine is proven identical, so a mismatch means the server's live *input assembly* diverged for that athlete/date — unverified, not yet trusted); the client keeps rendering its own local answer for that day, unchanged from pre-migration behavior. A fetch failure/timeout behaves the same way (fail-safe). **Once mismatch logs have been reviewed and are clean, remove the match-gate in `fetchServerPrescription` so the server is trusted unconditionally** — that is the one remaining step to close this note out entirely.
  - **`weekAhead()` is a deliberate, permanent-for-now exception:** it stays fully client-computed. The endpoint answers for one date; calling it 7× sequentially for the week-at-a-glance view would trade an instant local computation for real latency on a secondary/planning screen. A batched multi-day endpoint is the right fix if/when this matters — not a blocker to switching `today` (the primary, most-read, safety-relevant surface) first.
  - **Population-aware ACWR risk-zone classification — investigated 2026-07-08, found to be MOOT today:** the youth/RTP evidence presets (`evidence-presets.ts`) that motivated this are **never actually selected** — `EvidenceConfigService.activePresetId` is a hardcoded signal defaulting to `adult_flag_competitive_v1`, and `setPreset()` has **zero callers anywhere in the app** (verified by grep). So today, every athlete — including one on a return-to-play plan — is always scored against the adult baseline; the "RTP athlete shown danger, server thinks safe" divergence the Batch-1 drift guard flagged is a real *architectural* risk (if the preset selector is ever wired up) but not a *live* one. The youth/RTP presets + `setPreset()` are effectively **orphaned**, same pattern as `exercisedb`/`session-load-import` — flagged here rather than silently left to look load-bearing.
  - Do not remove the client engine (`localPrescription`, the fallback + `weekAhead`'s source) — it stays load-bearing indefinitely, by design.
- Equipment inventory is stored on **`athlete_training_config.available_equipment`** (jsonb array of equipment ids) — reuse this column; do **not** use the dead/missing `athlete_recovery_profiles`/`equipment_items` tables. Catalogue of ids+labels lives as reference data, never hardcoded into logic.
- **Equipment gate (LAW):** the engine may only recommend a modality the player actually owns/has access to (no Normatec without compression boots, ever).
- **Modality triggers (data-driven):** compression boots → post high-load / ACWR spike / congested fixtures; massage gun → pre-session activation or post-session localized tightness; stretching/mobility → tightness, low-readiness, maintenance (always available, bodyweight); massage knives (IASTM) → persistent localized tightness; foam roller → general post-session soreness; physio referral → severity threshold crossed **or** recurrence.
- **Self-report → recalculation (Merlin loop, LAW):** a region tightness/soreness report (Wellness region selector or Merlin chat) is persisted to `daily_wellness_checkin.soreness_areas` **and** an active restriction in `athlete_injuries` (`recovery_status='active'`, `activity_restrictions[]`, short auto-expiry). The system **MUST recalculate** load/RPE/prescription — never silently keep the old plan. **Injury/physio precedence overrides training (LAW):** a relevant signal down-regulates/removes sprint/high-intensity work for the affected region regardless of the periodization plan (competition/taper safety branches still apply). Every override is deterministic and logged (region, severity, source; intent before→after).

### 5b. Daily-input forms — prefill & non-destructive submit (detailed law)

Canonical lifecycle for every recurring input form (wellness check-in, supplements, post-event participation, self-report, RPE/session log). A form that opens with hardcoded defaults **and** submits via upsert silently destroys saved data on a no-touch resubmit — **P0**.

1. **On open — fetch** the existing record for the period from the canonical table (verified name — `daily_wellness_checkin`, not `wellness_checkin`).
2. **Prefill** from SAVED values if a record exists; else a genuine empty/unset state — never fabricated mid-range defaults that look real.
3. **Distinguish unset from a real value** (touched/dirty flag or null state); gate submit on it where a default could be mistaken for a real entry.
4. **On submit — non-destructive:** never overwrite a saved column with an untouched default. Partial update of changed fields, or full prefill so the upsert carries real values. (`upsert_wellness_checkin` COALESCEs null→existing, so sending only touched fields is safe there.)
5. **Failure:** if the prefill fetch fails, surface it and **block the destructive submit** — do not fall back to defaults and write.

Reference implementations: the wellness check-in prefill `effect()` and `profile-edit.component.ts` prefill-from-GET. Append-only logs (hydration, training-session) must be **idempotent per natural key** (one `training_sessions` row per `user_id`+`session_date`+`session_type`) so a double submit updates rather than double-counts into ACWR.

Unfixed:

- **2026-07-09 — live-app fixes + squad monitoring (from field feedback).** Four issues off the live site: (1) **Squad monitoring** — NEW coach/physio squad-wide daily table (`/team-monitoring`, `team-monitoring.js` → `/api/team-monitoring`, linked from `/reports`). One row per active team_members athlete: pre-session wellness (sleep/energy/stress/soreness/readiness), internal load (RPE/min/sRPE), ACWR, readiness flags + action, and a MEAN row. **TWO gates, both reusing vetted code:** caller must hold a `HEALTH_DATA_ACCESS_ROLES` role (`getUserRole`), and each athlete row goes through `canCoachViewWellness` (the SAME per-athlete consent check as the single-athlete report) — no consent → name only, no health data. Live data is currently thin (1 team, 5 team_members, wellness on 2 athletes; `session_load`/`external_load_metrics` empty so no Catapult external-load columns — those appear if/when wearable data lands). 6 backend tests on the flag/mean logic. (2) **Merlin** was consent-gated with the only enable path buried in Settings → added an inline "Enable Merlin" button in the gated chat state (explicit consent via `updateAiProcessing(true)`, records the consent date). (3) **Today's Exercises** 1-exercise blocks stretched into giant empty cards (`align-items: start`) + dividers between exercises within a block. (4) Readiness/ACWR charts got the data-viz-audit a11y treatment earlier the same window.

- **2026-07-08 — reuse/duplication audit (jscpd-measured).** Whole-codebase duplication measured at **3.74%** (3,954 dup lines / 314 clones, tests excluded) — already low/healthy (industry "green" is <5%). Breakdown: ~41% is same-file internal repetition (deduping = intra-file abstraction, usually hurts readability → left); a large chunk is per-function Netlify router scaffold (OPTIONS/CORS/URL-parse preambles across ~15–20 entry files — inherent to the serverless entry-point pattern → left, not worth `createRouter()` indirection touching every endpoint's request path); the rest is coincidental structural similarity (similar query shapes, not shared logic → left). **Cleaned the genuine cross-file logic dupes only** (each verbatim-verified + tested): R1 `getTimeAgo` (supabase-client's private copy → the canonical `date-utils`); R2 `parseIso`/`validationError`/`str` (three event-write handlers — athlete-events/event-travel/event-games each carried copies → `utils/event-fields.js`); R3 `mapKnownHandlerError` (the invalid_json/integer-between catch chain → `utils/handler-errors.js`, 2 byte-identical exemplars migrated, ~23 structurally-varied ones left to adopt incrementally). **Deliberately left** (genuine but not worth the risk/reward): the profile-edit ↔ settings avatar-upload logic (~15L, touches 2 UI components); isometrics/plyometrics (only 2 callers and they return *different* response shapes — nested vs flat — so a factory would mask a real API-contract difference); the invitation-validity gate (validate-invitation has a DB side-effect accept-invitation lacks). **The one large remaining lever stays incremental:** ~98 backend functions still do ad-hoc validation vs the shared `validateInput` DSL (F7) — a campaign, not a batch, since each has a subtly different accept/reject contract.
- **2026-07-08 — codebase consistency audit (propagate the past-day patterns).** Swept the codebase for where the day's single-source/security patterns weren't yet applied. **Fixed:** (C1, safety) injury grade→severity-tier classification was triplicated — `normalizeSeverity` (F8 canonical), `active-injuries.js injuriesPainLevel` (own rank map), `calc-readiness.js` (inline `=== "severe" || "Grade 3"`); both consumers now route through `normalizeSeverity` (behavior-identical, drives RTP sizing + readiness penalty). (D1, correctness) `wellness.service.ts` typed the check-in `id` as `number` and mapped it via `Number()` → `NaN` for the actual `uuid`, silently breaking realtime UPDATE/DELETE matching; id → `string`. (B1) `resolveAgeYears` was the last stray re-implementation of `ageFromDob`'s month/day calc (F2); now delegates, keeping only its 16–80 CNS bound. (E1) `app.constants.ts TRAINING.ACWR_*` duplicated the evidence-preset ACWR thresholds (F9 canonical) but is used only for an ordering sanity-check, not live classification — added a drift-guard test locking the two together + a pointer comment. **Investigated, found NOT a bug (no change):** `trends.js`'s `athleteId` interpolated into a `.or()` is fully gated by the handler's `athleteId !== userId → 403` check before the query, so it can only ever be the caller's own trusted uuid (contrast the genuinely-exploitable free-text `knowledge-search` case, which WAS fixed). Other `.or()` sites interpolate server-resolved uuids/dates. No other `Number(uuid)` realtime bugs exist (channel/toast services use string ids).
- **2026-07-08 — wknw01 security extraction (surgical, not a branch merge).** The `claude/code-architecture-audit-wknw01` branch (14 commits, diverged 2026-06-29, −34k lines vs a pre-consolidation base) was **never merged** — a bulk merge would clobber the past week's consolidation + the F1–F8 audit work. Instead each of its security/data-loss findings was triaged against **current main + live DB** and only genuinely-missing fixes re-applied. **Already salvaged on main (verified, no action needed):** ai-chat youth-safety enforcement, the cross-user singleton-leak sign-out guard, password-recovery intent, `platform.service.ts` real `localStorage`, `calc-readiness` null-ACWR guard, `daily-protocol-rtp` injured-region filtering. **Live DB already correct (verified via Supabase MCP + `get_advisors`: 0 security-critical findings — no `rls_enabled_no_policy`/`security_definer_view`/`public_bucket`/`function_search_path_mutable`):** `physical_measurements_latest` has `security_invoker=true`, `proactive_checkins`/`team_activities` RLS policies present, community-media broad list policy dropped, anon/public execute revoked on `upsert_wellness_checkin`, `increment_training_points` locked to service_role. **Extracted (genuinely missing):** (1) `knowledge-search.js` interpolated raw `query` into a PostgREST `.or()` filter with no metacharacter sanitization — a live injection vector (the H3 hardening fixed the identical pattern in `smart-ai-service.js` but was never applied here); fixed + 3 tests. (2) Six DB security migration **files** (`20260701171132`/`171312`/`234056`/`20260702104954`/`20260703082528`/`082611`) that were applied **live** but never committed to version control — backfilled verbatim (each verified to match live state) so a fresh `db reset` reproduces the fixed state instead of re-opening the holes. **Remaining WARN-level advisories (pre-existing, not this pass):** 69× `authenticated_security_definer_function_executable` (the accepted SECURITY DEFINER RPC pattern) + 1× `auth_leaked_password_protection` (an Auth-dashboard toggle, not code). **Correctness bug found, deferred to the consistency audit:** `wellness.service.ts` coerces the check-in `uuid` id via `Number()` → `NaN`, silently breaking realtime UPDATE/DELETE matching (`NaN !== NaN`).
- **2026-07-08 — full rules/single-source-of-truth audit.** `docs/generated/live-schema.snapshot.json` had drifted (last captured 2026-07-06, before the entire monitoring-feature migration set + the audit-fix migrations) — `docs-regen` was silently working off a stale snapshot showing 169 tables and 11 "unknown table ref" warnings for live tables it didn't know about. **Fixed:** re-captured the snapshot from live (`information_schema` via Supabase MCP) and regenerated `supabase-types.ts` — now 181 tables, 0 unknown refs, 0 stale-in-types. Also found and fixed: (1) this doc's own header/§1/§2/§3 stats were stale (188→181 tables, Angular 21→22, 224→249 migration files, 113→120 functions); (2) the entire **Athlete monitoring** feature (12 migrations, 5 endpoints, the `monitoring-report` screen, shipped 2026-07-07) was never added to §4 — a direct §8 violation, now added; (3) §7's auth-hardening note described the OLD vulnerable state (anon-executable SECURITY DEFINER fns) as required/permanent — that was fixed live 2026-07-07/08 (revoked, verified 0 anon-executable), but the doc still told a future reader those fns "must stay executable," which would have meant re-introducing the hole if followed; corrected. (4) Law #6 ("server is canonical for the engine") directly contradicted §5a ("prescription is owned by the client") — not a new bug, but the doc asserted both without reconciling them; §5a now states the honest current split and points at the in-flight migration (§5a note, updated live as that migration progresses).
- **Calculation-ownership audit (2026-07-08, see `docs/ground-truth/calculation-ownership-audit-2026-07-08.md`).** Full per-metric sweep (readiness, ACWR, periodization, RPE/workload, Hooper, weight, injury flags, CNS classification). Found and fixed: a dead ACWR spike-cap service that wrote to a table dropped months earlier (illusory safety feature, deleted); two divergent high-CNS session classifiers (unified onto one, now incl. `competition`); a hand-copied ACWR risk-zone block in `load-management.js` (now reads `ACWR_RISK_ZONES`); an inconsistent inline workload formula across 3 log paths (now `utils/session-load.js`). **Still open, deliberately not auto-fixed:** `wellness-checkin.js`'s check-in-time readiness estimate and `calc-readiness.js`'s composite readiness are two distinct, independently-tuned formulas (not a mechanical duplicate — the check-in formula has a deliberate scale-inversion safeguard the composite lacks); true unification is a sports-science decision needing a before/after delta, not something to merge blindly. The canonical wellness-index scorer was extracted to `utils/readiness-score.js` as the safe partial fix.

- **2026-07-07 — UI/UX audit close-out + doc consolidation.** Actioned the safe, bounded items from the retired `UIUX_MODERNIZATION_AUDIT.md`: removed 3 zero-reference production deps (`chart.js`/`html2canvas`/`jspdf`); made the Stats "Performance" section an honest "will appear" state (no CTA the athlete can't act on — combine-test logging isn't built); made the Today readiness card fully tappable to `/wellness`; added a short-viewport (`max-height: 700px`) collapse of the decorative `.screen-hero` images so content leads on landscape phones. The two one-off analysis docs (`UIUX_MODERNIZATION_AUDIT.md`, `V2_PROPOSAL.md` — the latter's V2.0–V2.4 flagship already shipped) were **deleted and consolidated into `docs/BACKLOG.md`** (a slim living list of un-built work + the "don't build" guidance). Ground truth stays here; the backlog is only not-yet-built.

- **2026-07-07 — Angular-22 code-audit fixes** (the audit doc itself was deleted once actioned; this is the durable record). The Angular app audited clean overall (44/44 OnPush, fully `@if/@for` + signals + `inject()`, ~zero `any`); these were the actionable findings:
  - **Systemic cross-user-leak guard (root-singleton state):** `SupabaseService.signOut()` now does a full `window.location.assign("/login")` after clearing the session + SW cache — a reload resets every root singleton's in-memory state by construction, so no user-scoped service can serve a previous user's cached role/injuries/settings to the next sign-in on a shared device. This is the systemic backstop for the whole leak class; the per-service `userId`-keyed guards on `team-membership`/`periodization` (2026-07-07 above) remain as defense-in-depth.
  - **Regression tests for the shipped security fixes:** new specs lock `team-membership.service` (cross-user cache isolation), `platform.service` (real localStorage/sessionStorage round-trip), and `auth-flow-data.service` (password-recovery intent set→detect→expire). +9 tests (Angular suite 461 → 470).
  - **Shared typed event helper:** `shared/utils/event.utils.ts` (`eventValue`/`eventNumber`/`eventChecked`) replaces the `val(e)`/`num(e)` helpers that were copy-pasted into 3 components. Inline `$any($event.target)` in templates left as-is (idiomatic, low-value churn).
  - **`standalone: true` removed** from all 44 components (redundant default since Angular v19).
  - **Design-token inline violations:** of 303 inline `style="…"`, only **3** used hardcoded non-token values (`gap:4px` ×3 → `var(--s-1)`); fixed. The other ~300 are token-compliant one-liners — deliberately **not** migrated to SCSS (CSS-specificity churn with real visual-regression risk, unverifiable without the Linux-baselined visual suite, for ~zero benefit since they already use tokens).
  - **Deferred (not done, logged):** decompose the large services (`periodization` 2.6k / `channel` 1.3k / `acwr` 1.3k — periodization is well-sectioned + 94 tests, split is behavior-neutral-but-optional; `channel.service` is untested and the more urgent one); backfill tests for `channel.service`/`privacy-settings.service`; adopt `httpResource()` on read paths incrementally.

- **2026-07-07 — security & data-loss fixes salvaged from the stale audit branch** (`origin/claude/code-architecture-audit-wknw01`, code-only — no live-DB migration). That branch (14 commits, forked from the pre-session June-29 main, a −34k-line dead-code sweep) overlaps heavily with work already merged (v2.4, cleanup, archaudit); a blind merge would resurrect what main deliberately moved past. Instead its still-live fixes were extracted surgically and verified against current main. Its DB/RLS commits were already superseded (the `proactive_checkins`/`team_activities` RLS-gap fix + storage-listing fix landed live via v2.4 as `20260703082528`/`20260703082611`; 8/10 of its migration-reconciliation files are already on main). What was genuinely missing and is now applied:
  - **Cross-user data leak on shared devices (P0)** — `team-membership.service.ts` + `periodization.service.ts` are root singletons that outlive in-SPA sign-out (no page reload), so a second user signing in within the 30s cache TTL got the FIRST user's role/team/injuries/settings/travel. Fixed with a `userId`-keyed cache guard (`cached.userId === user.id`, `_loadPromiseUserId`) and a userId-change `effect()` that reloads recent-sessions/injuries/settings/travel and clears them on sign-out. **Injury leak was safety-relevant** (unguarded sprint work for an athlete with a real injury, or a phantom guard for one without).
  - **Password reset permanently broken** — `supabase.service.ts`'s `PASSWORD_RECOVERY` handler never called `AuthFlowDataService.markPasswordRecoveryIntent()`, and `platform.service.ts`'s storage helpers were pure in-memory (never touched real `localStorage`/`sessionStorage`), so the recovery intent couldn't survive the reset link's fresh tab. Both fixed.
  - **RTP not filtered by injured region** — `daily-protocol-rtp.js` promised "focus on areas NOT injured" but never filtered exercise pools; could prescribe a hamstring stretch to a hamstring injury. Now filters via the exported `isExerciseSafeForInjuries` (rehab block deliberately exempt).
  - **AI youth-safety never enforced on the streaming/SSE path** — `ai-chat.js` checked a non-existent `isYouthBlocked` field (always false); now uses `youthRestrictions?.isBlocked` matching the already-fixed non-streaming path (block + parent notification). Request-race guards added to `wellness.service.ts`/`readiness.service.ts`; optimistic-rollback on failed coach-alert ack; gameday hydration total load.
  - **Partial settings save wiped every other field (P0 data-loss, §5b violation)** — `player-settings.js`'s `athlete_training_config` upsert wrote every optional field unconditionally (`value || default`), so saving only position reset season calendar/equipment/etc. to defaults. Fixed with the conditional-spread pattern (`x !== undefined ? {…} : {}`) `teamTrainingDays` already used; age-derived fields only recompute when `birthDate` is in the payload. +2 regression tests. _(From `24c7bdf3`; its bundled season-calendar-region-presets feature was NOT taken — it overlaps the archaudit season calendar already on main.)_
  - **Not taken (superseded/divergent):** the branch's dead-code sweep, 7-stage season calendar, 77→286 exercise-library expansion, and migration renames are already done differently on main. **Still open:** the branch remains unmerged; nothing else from it is planned. Its remaining value is exhausted by the above.

- **2026-07-06 — dead-schema cleanup (applied live via Supabase MCP; migrations `20260706185636`, `20260706185652`, `20260706190823`):**
  - **19 dead tables dropped** (all 0 rows, zero references in app code, views, or function bodies; verified by two independent audits): `video_assignments`, `video_bookmarks`, `video_curation_status`, `video_watch_history`, `video_playlists` (shelved video-library family — `training_videos` is the live one), `weekly_training_analysis`, `player_game_summary`, `coach_film_sessions`, `coach_film_tags`, `coach_playbook_plays`, `player_development_goals`, `player_development_notes`, `player_skill_assessments`, `ai_feedback` (superseded by live `ai_response_feedback`), `ai_review_queue`, `consent_change_log`, `event_lineups`, `team_preferences`, `coach_athlete_assignments`. Base tables 188 → 169. DDL snapshot for the 10 that had no local migration file: `backups/20260706_dropped_dead_tables_ddl.json`.
  - **Dropped-in-error, restored same session:** `event_games` + `athlete_travel_log` were initially dropped on main-only evidence while the Tournament Mode v2.x code was still on its unmerged branch — restored verbatim (0 rows, so schema restore = full restore) incl. `event_participation.game_id`. The v2.x branch has since been merged to main (2026-07-06), closing that gap. **Lesson recorded: zero-reference checks must cover unmerged branches, not just main.**
  - **`coach_athlete_assignments` was a dead authorization path:** referenced only inside 3 other tables' RLS policies (`execution_logs`, `readiness_scores`, `session_version_history`) as an OR-branch, but nothing ever inserted a row — the branch could never grant access. Policies recreated without it (behavior-neutral); real staff access is the `team_members` path.
  - **10 missing FK constraints added** (all pre-verified: matching types, 0 orphans; `user_id→users` uses ON DELETE CASCADE to align with GDPR hard-delete): `event_availability.user_id`, `execution_logs.exercise_id` (SET NULL), `ff_exercise_mappings.exercisedb_exercise_id`, `flag_pull_stats/passing_stats/receiving_stats.game_event_id`, `nutrition_reports.team_id` (SET NULL), `proactive_checkins.user_id`, `readiness_scores.user_id`, `team_activities.team_id`.
  - **Wrong-wired, FK impossible (varchar keys) — NOT fixed, needs a deliberate type migration:** the legacy game-stats family stores keys as `character varying` against int/uuid parents: `games.team_id` (varchar vs `teams.id` uuid), `game_events.game_id`/`team_id`, `flag_pull_stats`/`passing_stats`/`receiving_stats.game_id` (varchar vs `games.id` int), `situational_stats.user_id`, `user_notification_preferences.user_id` (varchar vs `users.id` uuid). Code joins these in JS, so nothing errors at runtime — but no referential integrity is possible until the columns are retyped. `position_specific_metrics.position_id` has no parent table at all.
  - **Rules tables are curated but unwired:** `taper_rules` (5 rows), `weather_substitution_rules` (8), `contraindication_rules` (22) hold real methodology content, but the engine uses hardcoded constants (`TAPER_CONFIG` etc.) instead of reading them. `prescription_audit_log` (0 rows, same reconstructed feature family) has no writer yet — kept. Wiring these is a deliberate engine change (the periodization preserve-list applies), not a mechanical fix.
  - **Migration-file drift:** the 2026-07-06 v2.x merge restored the local migration file set (224 files + this cleanup's 3 = 227). Residual known mismatch: the six v2.x files carry authoring timestamps (`20260702120000`…`20260704100000`) that differ from their applied versions (`20260703024214`…`20260703175554`) — same benign class as the 2026-06-25 reconciliation cases.
  - **Over-broad staff RLS observation (pre-existing, unchanged):** the recreated `readiness_scores`/`session_version_history` SELECT policies grant any active `physiotherapist`/`medical_staff`/`admin`/`owner` in ANY team read access to ALL rows (not scoped to shared-team). API-layer consent gating (`coach-core.js` blocked set) is the effective control; tightening RLS to shared-team scope would be defense-in-depth — a deliberate change, logged here rather than snuck in.

See `docs/v2/V2.4-global-tiers.md`.
- **`tournament_day_plans`, `warmup_protocols` — dead scaffolding, not cleaned up.** Found via V2.4's live-schema introspection: both tables are live, zero rows, zero code references (`tournament_day_plans` conceptually predates and overlaps V2.0's `event_games`). Not dropped — no destructive action taken without more context on their history. Flag for a future cleanup pass.

- **`competitions`/`competition_events` have zero write endpoints.** The competition-tier engine work (V2.4) can fully represent a continental/world/olympic team event, but nothing in the app can create one — a team event's tier is DB-only to set today. Athlete-entered national-team commitments (`athlete_events.tier`) have a full UI path; team-scheduled events don't.

- **2026-06-09 audit — resolved same day** (cross-team session mutation fix: see Ledger):
  - ~~Weather fake location~~ **Fixed**: `weather.js` resolves the caller's team `home_city` (geocoded) when no coords passed; no location → explicit `available:false`, never a default city. Client guard skips on unavailable. San Francisco defaults deleted. Team home_city is set via `teams.home_city` (no settings UI yet — set via SQL/roster admin).
  - ~~No unique constraint on training_sessions natural key~~ **Fixed**: `training_sessions_user_date_type_key` UNIQUE (user_id, session_date, session_type) + `log_training_session` RPC now ON CONFLICT upserts (COALESCE, non-destructive). Migration `20260609192100`.
  - ~~Periodization phase from global `is_active` program~~ **Fixed**: `training-plan.js` + `smart-training-recommendations.js` resolve via the athlete's `player_programs` assignment (prefers `current_phase_id`).
  - ~~Fabricated RPE in load math~~ **Fixed**: `training-plan.js`/`daily-training.js` ACWR now delegate to canonical `utils/acwr.js` (workload ?? rpe×min, missing → excluded). Client `acwr.service` excludes load-less sessions instead of zero-counting.
  - ~~Client ACWR EWMA divergence~~ **Fixed**: client mirrors server (oldest→newest fold, uncoupled 21d chronic, λ=2/(N+1)); evidence presets updated.
  - ~~RPE null-overwrite on resubmit~~ **Fixed**: update branch only writes provided fields, workload recomputed from merged (new ?? saved) pair.
  - ~~`compute-acwr` multi-team `maybeSingle()`~~ **Fixed**: staff-team × athlete-membership intersection across ALL memberships.
- **2026-06-09 audit — closed out (second pass):**
  - ~~Multi-team `limit(1)`/`maybeSingle()` in coach-core + team-activity-resolver~~ **Fixed**: `getCoachTeamId` is deterministic (most recent active membership) and accepts an explicit team — `?teamId=` on the coach GET lanes, `teamId` in POST payloads — validated against the caller's staffed memberships. `team-activity-resolver` searches activities across ALL of the athlete's active teams (old `maybeSingle()` errored on >1 membership → silent "no activity"); newest created activity wins.
  - ~~No spec tests for injury/CNS guards~~ **Fixed**: `prescribeFor — injury guard` suite added (severe/moderate/minor scaling, no-op cases, competition-day immunity, injury-beats-weather precedence); CNS spacing suite landed with the feature commit.
  - **Orphaned ghost-lane tests removed** (officials/equipment/depth-chart/scouting/program-cycles/season-reports/tournament-calendar/push ×10 files + absence-request & research-sync cases); stale `user-profile` pg-Pool mock rewritten for the supabase client.

- **2026-06-10 — engine personalization & safety pass (athlete-driven brief):** the prescription engine is now age-, position-, region-, season- and travel-aware. All changes push in the _safe_ direction (more conservative); adversarially verified.
  - **Nutrition**: `CARB_PER_KG` periodised to energy expenditure — a short sprint day is 4.5 g/kg (was a flat 6 → 480g for 80kg), pre-game/game-day stay elevated (6/7). Heat now adds +0.5L fluid (was documented, never applied).
  - **Injury regions** (`athlete-injuries.js restrictionsFor`): added **soleus** + patella/adductor/tibialis/plantar/ITB/tendon to lower-limb, a **core/trunk** category, and an **upper-body** category; an unrecognised region now **fails safe** (restricts sprint + high-intensity + throwing). The client injury guard derives `restrictsThrowing` so an upper-body issue pulls throwing/snapping without touching running.
  - **Age** (`cnsRecoveryHoursForAge`): CNS recovery window <35→48h, 35–39→60h, 40+→72h (monotonic, floored, from `date_of_birth`).
  - **Position** (`withPositionEmphasis`): QB/WR-DB/center accessory-prehab emphasis (additive only — never changes intent/load), surfaced on Today; throwing-restriction overrides QB/center into a protect-the-arm message.
  - **Tournament congestion**: `peakDayGameCount` now a conservative worst-day estimate (`ceil(games/(days-1))`, not the average — averaging fail-open fixed); ≥3 games/day trips heavy-density de-load regardless of the 10/14d total.
  - **Season phases**: added **peak** (sharp/low-volume) and **postseason** (regeneration); split seasons (multiple windows incl. a mid-season off gap) resolve per day via `macroPhaseFor`. Onboarding picker offers Off/Pre/In/Peak/Post-season.
  - **Travel**: `daily_wellness_checkin.travel_hours` + extended `upsert_wellness_checkin` RPC + a wellness chip control + a bounded **readiness penalty** in `calc-readiness` (1–2h −2, 3–5h −4, ≥6h −8; subtract-only).
  - **Knowledge base**: 30 → **104** evidence-based, merlin-approved entries (hamstrings, knees, glutes, core, isometrics→plyometrics, warm-ups, rest, age bands, QB/WR/DB/center demands, travel, nutrition periodisation). Adult-male-16+ only.
  - Calibration constants (carb g/kg, CNS hours, congestion threshold, travel penalty) are documented in-code as **starting points** for the coach to tune against team history.
  - **Per-game ACWR load**: `calc-readiness` now injects an estimated per-game internal load (~350 AU, tunable) for PAST games from the schedule spine into the daily-load map (multi-day events spread across days; per-day MAX(logged, estimate) so it never lowers a day and avoids double-count), so a tournament's acute load/ACWR rises instead of reading falsely safe.
  - ~~**Open / deferred:** QB throw-count / center snap-count → quantified load gating (needs the throw/snap logs wired + the coach's dosing thresholds)~~ **Reassessed 2026-06-25**: not actually a "needs building" gap — `netlify/functions/qb-throwing.js` (424 lines, arm-care endpoint, writes `training_sessions.throw_au`/`throw_count`) and the `api.service.ts` route table already exist but have **zero callers anywhere in the Angular app** — orphaned, same pattern as the ExerciseDB lane in §4. No UI ever logs a throw count, so there's no data for the engine to gate on regardless of dosing-threshold decisions. Region-specific prehab/return-to-play _progression_ engine (the knowledge base covers the education); season leap-day (Feb 29) + user-declared overlapping windows are minor edge cases. `ensure_public_user_profile` has a pre-existing `last_name` NOT NULL quirk for email-only auth users (affects all wellness writes for such users).

- **2026-06-10 — architecture refactor + COMPOSE wiring (two engines clarified):** an audit confirmed `periodization.service.ts` (Angular, **intent/targets** — Today/Training/Gameday) and `daily-protocol.js` (backend, **exercise realization**) are TWO engines. Decision: **compose** (intent layer authoritative, daily-protocol realizes exercises for it). Do NOT delete daily-protocol (a 10-module, routed, tested engine; the earlier "dead code" read was wrong).
  - **Class 3 (config extraction, behaviour-neutral):** `TAPER_CONFIG` (taper windows/targets centralised); `baseTargets` (in-season) vs `BUILD_TARGET_OVERRIDES` (pre-season heavier on light intents) — resolves audit **M1** (the divergence is deliberate, now explicit); `INJURY_RESPONSE` (injury caps). Each verified byte-identical against the 85-anchor periodization preserve-list.
  - **Class 1 / finding 1.1:** a practice day on a recovery phase is now honoured at recovery intensity (a `recovery` row in `PRACTICE_PHASE_MODIFIERS`) instead of being dropped; intent is data-driven per row.
  - **COMPOSE backend:** `daily-protocol /generate` optionally takes `{intent, intentLabel, position}`; `mapIntentToSession` + `positionFlagsFor` realize exercises for that intent and fix the `isQB`/center bug (raw `'qb'` ≠ `'quarterback'`). **Backward-compatible** — no intent → legacy path byte-unchanged (8 legacy + 8 mapping tests).
  - **COMPOSE client:** `ProtocolService` posts the intent → Training renders the realized blocks as **block-cards** (re-skin adapted from `todays-practice-redesign-mockup.html`, mapped to app tokens, responsive 1-col→2-up@768). Render contract **verified against real data** (3 protocols / 33 exercises; block_types + exercise fields match the model).
  - **Status:** all of the above is **🔧 BUILT, NOT DEPLOYED**, and the compose stack's intent→exercise _quality_ is **not yet e2e-verified** (needs a live intent-passed `/generate`). Coach guide shipped: `FlagFit-Coach-Guide.docx` (+ internal appendix).
  - **2026-06-23 closeouts:** ~~Class 1 `resolveDayType→applyModifiers` pipeline~~ **closed** — the day-type→`PRACTICE_PHASE_MODIFIERS` pipeline (incl. finding 1.1: practice on a recovery phase honoured at recovery intensity) is now **locked by 7 spec tests** in `periodization.service.spec.ts` (94/94 green). ~~Settings position-picker discoverability~~ **closed** — a primary-position picker is now in Settings → Prefs (`settings.component`), saving `primaryPosition` to `/api/player-settings` and refreshing the prescription engine; labels single-sourced from `POSITION_VOLUME`.
  - **2026-06-24 closeouts:** ~~intent→exercise _quality_ e2e verification~~ **verified** against the live stack — `/api/daily-protocol/generate` with an `intent` payload realizes a distinct, intent-correct session per intent (strength→37 ex / sprint→12 / recovery→11 / practice→`practice_day`). ~~Class 2 schema cleanup~~ **done** — `deprecate_dead_fields` phase 2 (`20260624074022`) DROPPED the two `*_deprecated` columns from phase1 after live verification of zero code/view/function/policy/index dependency (dead data: 2× stale `[1,2,4,5,6]`, 16× `0.5`). ~~deploy~~ **not a manual step** — Netlify auto-publishes `main` → production on every merge (rollback: `netlify rollback`).
  - **Still remaining:** Class 2's _deferred_ fields (`daily_routine`/`current_limitations`/`flag_practice_schedule`/`max_sessions_per_week`) stay — phase1 found them to be intended features, not dead. ~~Migration-file hygiene (the timestamp-drifted duplicate files in §6 below) is unaddressed.~~ **Partly addressed 2026-07-01** — the two concrete missing-file gaps are fixed (see below); 9 pre-2026-06 out-of-band files remain untracked in the applied ledger (see below), left as-is.

- **2026-06-23 — load-domain + design-system drift sweep (this session's PRs):**
  - **Schema-drift CI clean slate:** all 44 schema-drift findings resolved (baseline 0); the CI guard (`scripts/check-schema-drift.mjs`) blocks new drift. New tables this window: `team_activities`, `proactive_checkins`; `training_sessions.throw_au` etc.
  - **Canonical load model unified:** `coach-core.js` no longer re-implements ACWR/readiness (was a simple-ratio + fabricated `1.0`/`85`) — both coach paths now use the canonical EWMA engine (`utils/acwr.js`) and the persisted `readiness_scores`. **Consent:** coach-facing readiness is now gated by the blocked set (no longer leaks a consent-blocked athlete's score).
  - **Gameday/tournament load:** `record_event_participation` RPC now **distributes** a multi-day tournament's load across its days (was lumped on day 1) and dedups any same-date competition session, so the two gameday paths can't double-count acute load.
  - **Recovery-block cap enforced:** `buildProtocolDecisionContext` now caps `adjustedLoadTarget` by an active `recovery_blocks.max_load_percent` (was set but never applied to the prescription).
  - **`calculated_readiness` scale:** `wellness-logs.js` now writes 0–100 (was 1–10 into a 0–100 column that `ai-chat`/`coach-core` read).
  - **Week-ahead fix:** `periodization.weekAhead()` applies today's readiness/ACWR only to today (mirrors the weather guard) — future days resolve to their phase-driven plan instead of 7 frozen recovery days.
  - **Design system — Phase E token migration:** the TS color bridge (`design-tokens.util.ts`, `app.constants.ts`) + `index.html` were emitting the **old** CSS var names (`--ds-primary-green`, `--space-*`, …) that no stylesheet defines → empty strings at runtime (broken chart colors, white flash, Poppins). Migrated to Phase E tokens (`--accent`/`--good`/`--s-*`); `index.html` now links Space Grotesk + Plus Jakarta Sans (CSP updated for Google Fonts), dark-default boot CSS. Source of truth: `scss/tokens/_tokens.scss` (`--accent #00E07A`). ~~Deferred: `e2e/color-contrast.spec.ts` rework~~ **Done 2026-06-23** — the spec was reworked off the dead PrimeNG/"white-on-green" rules to enforce **WCAG AA contrast ratios** and lock the ink-on-accent pairing (mint `#00E07A` + `--on-accent` ink ≈ 11:1; white-on-mint would fail). ~~Still deferred: visual-regression baseline regen~~ **Done 2026-06-25** — all 23 baselines in `angular/e2e/__snapshots__/visual-regression.spec.ts/` re-captured against the live app (post-PrimeNG-removal, post-Phase-E tokens); fixed a stale `loginToApp` helper (`waitForURL` only matched `/dashboard|onboarding/`, app now lands on `/today` post-login) that was silently blocking the desktop-suite captures; deleted the orphaned `primeng-card.png` baseline (component no longer exists, no test referenced it).

- **2026-07-01 — travel-day feature audit + fix pass (bugs found on the unmerged `feat/travel-hotel-info` branch, before merge):**
  - **Client/server phase-resolver mismatch (Today vs. Week-Ahead):** `periodization.weekAhead()` computed day-0's phase via the client's own `resolvePhase` instead of the server-computed `snap.currentPhase` the `today` signal uses — the two resolvers key off different day-of-week sources (local vs UTC) and could disagree for a few hours around UTC midnight, showing a different phase/prescription on Today vs. the first Week-Ahead tile for the same day. **Fixed**: day 0 now reuses `snap.currentPhase`.
  - **Travel day bypassed the ACWR-danger/readiness-collapse safety guards:** the travel branch returned before reaching guards explicitly commented "overrides phase — safety first" / "regardless of phase". **Fixed**: travel-day handling moved after both guards (matches how team-practice already deferred to them); 3 new spec tests lock this (`periodization.service.spec.ts` — "prescribeFor — travel day").
  - **Unreachable travel/game-day badge:** Today's and Training's hero-band badge checked `recoveryEmphasis` before `intent`, so the "travel day"/"game day" labels could never render (both intents always carry a "high"/"critical" emphasis) — Today and Training could also show different labels for the same day. **Fixed**: intent-specific labels now checked first in both components.
  - **Training-points feature silently no-op:** `training-complete.js` inserted into `user_achievements`, a read-only view — every write threw and was swallowed. **Fixed**: new `increment_training_points` RPC upserts `player_training_stats.total_points` directly (SECURITY DEFINER, service_role-only).
  - **Onboarding race condition:** `player-settings.js` did check-then-insert instead of an atomic upsert; concurrent submits could double-insert and silently drop data. **Fixed**: now calls the existing `ensure_public_user_profile` RPC (atomic INSERT…ON CONFLICT) then an unconditional UPDATE.
  - **`ensure_public_user_profile` `last_name` NULL bug — confirmed and fixed**: `last_name` derived via substring-after-first-space was NULL for any email-only auth user (no space in the source string); `users.last_name` is NOT NULL, so the profile-ensure insert threw, blocking every RPC that calls it first (`upsert_wellness_checkin`, onboarding, etc). Fixed with a `COALESCE(..., 'Account')` floor.
  - **Other fixes**: dead `travel: "morning_mobility"` video-category mapping (no such category exists) → `mobility`; staff create-event form never set `competitions.level`, so coaches had no way to flag an international/continental/world fixture → added a level picker; `wellness-checkin.js` was missing `travelHours` range validation (bad input surfaced as a 500, not 422) → added.
  - **DB hygiene**: 2 prod-applied migrations had no local file (`storage_community_media_rls`, `add_hotel_info_to_competition_events`, the latter existing locally under the wrong timestamp) — both fixed/renamed to their real applied versions; regenerating docs also surfaced `sponsors` (live + used, reconstructed) and `video_bookmarks`/`weekly_training_analysis` (live, unreferenced by any current code — left as flagged DRIFT, a keep-or-drop call for the team, not auto-dropped). 9 pre-2026-06 out-of-band local files remain untracked in the applied-migration ledger (idempotent, low blast-radius per the 2026-06-25 reconciliation) — not re-registered, since doing so without re-verifying each against live state risked writing an inaccurate ledger entry.
  - **Security/perf**: `upsert_wellness_checkin` was anon-executable via a leftover default PUBLIC grant (self-guarded, not exploitable) → revoked. `prescription_audit_log`/`rtp_prescription_approvals`/`team_season_phases` (reconstructed tables) missed the schema-wide RLS auth-initplan optimization pass → applied; `physical_measurements`'s 5 percentage columns were `numeric(4,2)` (same 100.00-overflow class as the fixed readiness-score bug) → widened to `numeric(5,2)`; `rtp_prescription_approvals.approved_by`/`daily_protocol_id` FKs were unindexed → indexed.
  - `supabase-types.ts` and `docs/generated/*` regenerated; `live-schema.snapshot.json` bumped to 2026-07-01 (this also fixed genuinely stale doc content dated 2026-06-23 that pre-dated the 2026-06-25 migration reconciliation and never got a re-regen).

## 7. Runbooks & Security (operational — folded from the old RUNBOOKS, stale traps fixed)

Canonical: health = `/api/health`; env vars = `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY` (primary) **or** `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` (fallback) — code checks both via `||` in `supabase-client.js`; set either one; project ref `grfjmnjpzvknmsxrwesx`. `workout_logs` no longer exists — never reference it in ops queries.

**Health / triage.** `curl -s https://<site>/api/health | jq` → expect `{status:"healthy"}`; `degraded`=investigate, `unhealthy`=incident. Logs: `netlify logs:function <name> --last 50`, `netlify logs:build`. Severity: SEV-1 outage <15m, SEV-2 major feature <1h, SEV-3 degraded <4h, SEV-4 cosmetic next-day.

**Deployment rollback.** Fast: `netlify rollback` → re-check `/api/health`. Specific: `netlify deploys --json | jq '.[:10]'` → `netlify deploys:publish <ID>`. Code: `git revert <hash> --no-edit && git push origin main`. Rule: cause unclear after 5–15m → roll back. **Migrations are forward-only** (no down migrations); for DB-caused regressions restore from a pre-migration dump, not `supabase migration down`.

**Backup / restore.** Pre-migration: `supabase db dump -f backups/pre_migration_$(date ...).sql`. Record row counts before/after for `users`, `training_sessions`, `player_programs`. Restore full: `psql "<conn>" < file.sql` (destructive); selective: `SET session_replication_role='replica';` → restore → `'origin'`. Env: `netlify env:list` (encrypted — has secrets); PITR is Supabase Pro-only; Storage buckets are NOT auto-backed-up.

**Account deletion.** request → soft delete (immediate, sessions revoked) → 30-day cancellable queue → hard delete. API `account-deletion.js` (`/api/account-deletion`); edge fn `supabase/functions/process-deletions` (pg_cron 3 AM); DB fns `initiate_account_deletion()`, `cancel_account_deletion()`, `process_hard_deletion(uuid)`, `get_deletions_ready_for_processing()`; tables `account_deletion_requests`, `privacy_audit_log`. Backlog: `SELECT count(*) FROM account_deletion_requests WHERE status='pending' AND scheduled_hard_delete_at<=now();` (warn>10, crit>50). Force: `SELECT process_hard_deletion('<uuid>');`. Auth cleanup: `supabase.auth.admin.deleteUser('<uuid>')`.

**Privacy incident.** GDPR: notify DPA within 72h of a high/medium-risk breach. Containment: rotate Netlify + `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, invalidate sessions, `UPDATE users SET is_active=false WHERE id='<suspect>'`. Correct live columns: `consent_access_log(user_id, accessed_by, access_type, data_category, accessed_at, reason, consent_given)`; `parental_consent` keyed by `minor_user_id`, gate on `status`/`verified_at`. AI opt-out check: `ai_chat_sessions` × `privacy_settings WHERE ai_processing_enabled=false`. Validate: `npm run test:privacy`.

**Auth hardening.** Native HIBP leaked-password check is a GoTrue setting (Dashboard → Auth → Providers → Email, or Management API `PATCH /v1/projects/<ref>/config/auth {"password_hibp_enabled":true}`) — NOT toggleable via SQL; the app-level edge fn does not clear the advisor. **Fixed 2026-07-07/08:** the ~10 SECURITY DEFINER RLS-helper fns (`ff_is_active_team_member`, `ff_is_team_staff`, `has_role`, `is_active_superadmin`, `auth_user_team_ids`, `can_view_player_performance`, …) were `anon`-executable via a leftover `PUBLIC` grant — `REVOKE ... FROM public, anon` was applied to all of them (`authenticated`/`service_role` keep direct grants, which RLS calls rely on). Verified live: `anon`-executable SECURITY DEFINER count = **0**. Any NEW definer fn → `REVOKE FROM public, anon; GRANT authenticated, service_role` from the start — do not rely on a bare PUBLIC default.

---

## 8. Contributing — the Ledger is the contract

**Any PR that adds or changes a table, endpoint, or feature MUST update §4 (Feature Status Ledger) in the same commit.** This is how we stop rebuilding what exists.

**Regenerating the generated sections** (after a schema or route change):

1. Refresh the live schema snapshot: re-run the Supabase introspection (via Supabase MCP `execute_sql` over `information_schema`) and overwrite `docs/generated/live-schema.snapshot.json` (keep its shape; bump `generatedOn`). Optionally regenerate `supabase-types.ts` too.
2. `npm run docs:regen` — rewrites `docs/generated/DATA_MODEL.md` + `ENDPOINTS.md` deterministically and re-stamps the date.
3. Commit the regenerated files with your change.

If a generated file shows new ⚠️ ghost-table refs or DRIFT, fix the cause or log it in §6 — do not hand-edit the generated files (they are overwritten).
